title: Ukraine immigration measures: Contact us - Canada.ca
content:
## Ukraine immigration measures: Contact us
We’ve created a phone line and a web form to prioritize immigration-related questions from
- people affected by the Russian invasion of Ukraine
- people contacting us on behalf of someone who is affected
This page only has contact information for questions about
For other questions, learn about how Canada is responding to the Russian invasion of Ukraine .
You can sign up to get the latest updates on
- immigration programs
- priority processing to help affected people and
- other information about the Russian invasion of Ukraine
Find out how to sign up .
If you use our web form, the information we need you to include depends on the reason you’re contacting us.
To make sure we prioritize your question:
- In the “ Tell us about your situation ” box, describe your situation, including what you need help with add the keyword Ukraine 2022 at the end of your description
- describe your situation, including what you need help with
- add the keyword Ukraine 2022 at the end of your description
To make sure we can find your application and prioritize your question:
- In the “ Tell us about your situation ” box, describe your situation, including what you need help with add the keyword Ukraine 2022 at the end of your description
- describe your situation, including what you need help with
- add the keyword Ukraine 2022 at the end of your description
- If you’re changing your contact information (like your address or email), include both the old and new information in your request.
We’ll only share information with you if the principal applicant has authorized you to get information about their application. Learn more .
To make sure we prioritize your question:
- Complete the form using the applicant’s name and date of birth.
- In the “ Tell us about your situation ” box, include your full name describe your situation, including what you need help with add the keyword Ukraine 2022 at the end of your description

- add the keyword UKF2023 at the end of your description
- If you’re changing your contact information (like your address or email), include both the old and new information in your request.
We’ll only share information with you if the principal applicant has authorized you to get information about their application. Learn more .
To make sure we prioritize your question:
- Complete the form using the applicant’s name and date of birth.
- In the “ Tell us about your situation ” box, include your full name describe your situation, including what you need help with add the keyword UKF2023 at the end of your description
- include your full name
- describe your situation, including what you need help with
- add the keyword UKF2023 at the end of your description
- If you’re changing contact information (like an address or email), include both the old and new information in your request.
There must be one of these forms on file, naming you as a representative:
- Use of a Representative (IMM 5476) form Spouses or common-law partners must also use this form.
- Spouses or common-law partners must also use this form.
- Authority to Release Personal Information to a Designated Individual (IMM 5475) form
If there isn’t, you must submit one of these forms with your request.
Call us at +1-613-321-4243
- Monday to Friday, 8:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. (Canada local time)
- Available both inside and outside Canada.
- We’ll accept charges for collect calls or calls with reverse charges.
Wait times may be longer due to call volumes.
